title: My Season of Ativan
description: "Both of my parents were in hospice, on opposite coasts. Then I found out that I had breast cancer."
hook: She was juggling her parents' deaths on opposite coasts when her own cancer diagnosis landed. A portrait of what breaking looks like in real time.

title: Does fine dining have a toxic chef problem?
description: One of the most acclaimed restaurants in the world, Noma, opened a pop-up in Los Angeles on March 11. It was supposed to be a joyous occasion for Noma head chef and co-founder René Redzepi and the staff, who relocated from Copenhagen, Denmark, for the sold-out 16-week stint. But Noma LA’s opening has been mired […]
hook: Noma's LA pop-up was supposed to be triumphant. Instead it exposed the toxicity baked into fine dining's entire power structure.

title: “Absolutely Not a Genre Film”: Julia Ducournau in Conversation with Robert Eggers on Alpha
description: “To me it’s not really a shift,” French writer-director Julia Ducournau tells filmmaker Robert Eggers on the
hook: Julia Ducournau and Robert Eggers talk about making a film so unsettling it refuses to be categorized—or tamed by genre expectations.

title: The One Thousand Blobcows Born Each Year
description: “I think amorphous globosus is an ideal form: animate yet inanimate, parasitic yet harmless, geological yet doughy, static yet reactive.” On formlessness, Beckett, and Ambera Wellman.
hook: On formlessness as a radical act: how Beckett and modern thinkers found freedom in the amorphous, the doughy, the resistant to shape.

title: Interview: Nadav Lapid on His Incendiary Post-October 7 Israeli Satire ‘Yes’
description:
Lapid discusses the necessity of shooting Yes while the carnage in Gaza unfolded.

The post Interview: Nadav Lapid on His Incend
hook: An Israeli director made a savage satire after October 7—Nadav Lapid on why incendiary art matters most when everything feels too raw to joke about.
